stem: A cone and a cylinder have the same radius $r$ and the same height $h$. If the volume of the cylinder is $150\pi$ cubic centimeters, what is the volume, in cubic centimeters, of the space inside the cylinder but outside the cone?
explanation: A cone inscribed in a cylinder with the same radius and height has volume $\frac{1}{3}$ of the cylinder. Cone volume: $\frac{1}{3}(150\pi) = 50\pi$. Remaining space: $150\pi - 50\pi = 100\pi$ cubic centimeters. The answer is A.

distractorRationale.B: Reports the cone volume itself instead of the remaining space: $\frac{1}{3}(150\pi) = 50\pi$.
distractorRationale.C: Multiplies the cylinder volume by 3 instead of dividing: $150\pi \times 3 = 450\pi$.
distractorRationale.D: Adds the cone volume to the cylinder volume instead of subtracting: $150\pi + 50\pi = 200\pi$.
